description: This 3D surface plot illustrates the calculated relationship between yield strength improvement (Δσ_P) and carbide precipitation parameters in Fe–Mn–C–Al low-density steel based on the Ashby-Orowan strengthening model. The X-axis represents carbide size (0–100 nm), the Y-axis represents volume fraction f (0–1.0%), and the Z-axis represents yield strength increment Δσ_P (0–900 MPa). The colored curved surface demonstrates that higher volume fractions and smaller particle sizes significantly enhance strengthening effects. Experimental data points for specific carbides are marked with citation references: TiC [19], VC [21], V(C,N) [19] (two points), V₄C₃ [20], NbC [19], and VC/Fe₃C [5], showing good agreement with the model predictions. As stated in the caption and text, the calculation employs the Ashby-Orowan equation (Δσ_p = 0.847·M·(1.2/π)·G·b·√(3/(2π))·(f^0.5/D)·ln(D/(2b))), indicating that fine, high-volume-fraction Ti-, V-, and Nb-containing carbides act as effective obstacles to dislocation sliding, while κ-carbides are sheared by dislocations and cannot provide continuous strengthening. This figure supports the design strategy of precipitation hardening in low-density steels.
